description: Move the preview-safe Exa program execution behind one idempotent Oban worker after shared provider budgets exist. Model run lifecycle as durable Ash-owned state with queued/running/completed/partial_failure/failed outcomes, query provenance, reserved and actual cost, candidate counts, program linkage, idempotency key, attempt history, and terminal diagnostics; do not hide authoritative run state in program metadata. Scheduled and manual launches must enqueue the same worker, and retries must not duplicate runs or candidates. Acceptance: tests cover DiscoverySchedulerWorker cadence evaluation/enqueue behavior and the new execution worker enqueue/perform/retry/abandoned-run recovery; production scheduling is enabled only through the budget-aware durable path.

comments.text: Starting after fx2.21 merged. Goal: one Ash-owned run lifecycle and one Oban execution path for both manual and scheduled launches, preserving preview-only semantics and budget idempotency.

Implementation evidence: Commercial.DiscoveryRun state machine owns queued/running/completed/partial_failure/failed lifecycle, provenance, budget key/cost, attempts, counts and diagnostics; manual and scheduled launches enqueue one unique DiscoveryRunWorker; retry and abandoned-running recovery tests pass; 120 commercial/acquisition tests plus codegen/format/compile/map checks pass.

title: Run Exa discovery programs durably through Oban
